title: Why PDF Pages Rotate Automatically (And How to Fix It)
description: Learn why PDF pages rotate automatically and discover simple solutions to fix and prevent this common issue on mobile and PC. Understand metadata, viewer settings, and scanning errors.

faq.question: Why do PDF pages rotate automatically when opened?
faq.answer: PDF pages rotate automatically due to orientation metadata embedded in the file, viewer settings that auto-rotate based on device position, or incorrect scanning orientation. The PDF format stores rotation information that some viewers apply automatically.

faq.question: How can I permanently fix rotated PDF pages?
faq.answer: Use an online PDF rotation tool to manually set the correct orientation for each page, then save the PDF. This permanently embeds the correct rotation metadata so all viewers display pages correctly.

faq.question: Why do PDF pages look fine on one device but rotated on another?
faq.answer: Different PDF viewers interpret rotation metadata differently. Some viewers ignore it, some apply it automatically, and others have their own rotation settings. This causes inconsistent display across devices.

faq.question: Can I prevent PDF pages from rotating automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, you can disable auto-rotation in your PDF viewer settings, use PDF editing tools to remove rotation metadata, or create PDFs with correct orientation from the beginning.

faq.question: Is it safe to use online tools to fix PDF rotation?
faq.answer: Yes, reputable online PDF rotation tools use secure connections and automatically delete files after processing. For sensitive documents, use offline software or browser-based tools that process files locally.

faq.answer: Yes, absolutely! You can rotate PDFs on Android without any app installation by using browser-based PDF tools, cloud services like Google Drive or OneDrive, email attachments, or Progressive Web Apps that work like native apps but don't require installation from the Play Store.

faq.question: Do browser-based PDF rotation tools work well on mobile?
faq.answer: Modern browser-based PDF tools are optimized for mobile devices with touch-friendly interfaces, responsive designs, and mobile-optimized upload/download processes. They work just as well as dedicated apps for basic operations like rotation.

faq.question: Is it safe to rotate PDFs using online tools on Android?
faq.answer: Reputable online PDF tools use HTTPS encryption and have clear privacy policies stating that files are automatically deleted after processing. For sensitive documents, look for tools that process files locally in your browser or use established services like Google Drive.

faq.question: What if I don't have internet connection?
faq.answer: Without internet, your options are limited. Some browser-based tools offer offline capabilities through Progressive Web Apps, or you can use built-in PDF viewers in file managers. For regular offline use, consider lightweight PDF apps that don't require extensive permissions.

faq.question: Can I rotate specific pages or only the entire PDF?
faq.answer: Most browser-based tools allow you to rotate individual pages, selected pages, or all pages at once. You typically get a page-by-page preview where you can rotate each page independently before downloading the corrected PDF.

faq.question: Which browsers work best for PDF rotation on Android?
faq.answer: Chrome, Firefox, Samsung Internet, and Microsoft Edge all work well. Chrome generally offers the best Progressive Web App support, while Firefox focuses on privacy. All modern Android browsers support the necessary web technologies for PDF manipulation.

faq.question: Are there file size limitations?
faq.answer: Most web tools support up to 50MB files on mobile. Cloud services like Google Drive have higher limits based on your storage plan. Email attachments are typically limited to 25MB. For very large files, consider using Wi-Fi connection.

faq.question: Can I rotate scanned PDF documents?
faq.answer: Yes, all these methods work with scanned PDFs. However, scanned documents are essentially images, so the rotation is applied to the entire image. Text recognition (OCR) features are not typically available in web-based rotation tools.

faq.question: What is the difference between rotating a PDF page and rotating an image?
faq.answer: Rotating a PDF page changes the orientation of a page inside a document while preserving text, layout, and structure. Rotating an image only affects a single picture file and does not retain document formatting like selectable text or multiple pages.

faq.question: Why does rotating an image not fix PDF rotation issues?
faq.answer: PDFs store rotation data differently from images. Rotating an image only changes pixels, while PDFs rely on page-level rotation metadata. This is why image rotation tools cannot permanently fix PDF orientation problems.

faq.question: When should I rotate a PDF instead of converting it to an image?
faq.answer: You should rotate a PDF directly when the document contains text, multiple pages, or needs to remain searchable and printable. Converting to images reduces quality and removes text selection.

faq.question: Does rotating a PDF affect file quality?
faq.answer: No, rotating PDF pages does not reduce quality because it only updates orientation metadata. Image rotation may cause quality loss if the file is re-encoded.

faq.question: Which is better for documents: PDF rotation or image rotation?
faq.answer: PDF rotation is better for documents, forms, and scanned files. Image rotation is only suitable for photos or single-image files.

faq.question: What format should I use for printing?

faq.answer: PNG screenshots are perfect quality but create larger files. JPG screenshots are smaller files that are easier to share. For most website screenshots that you're sharing or documenting, JPG works just fine and saves space.

faq.question: Can I convert multiple screenshots at once?
faq.answer: Yes, many conversion tools let you upload several screenshots and convert them all to JPG at the same time. This is handy when you've taken multiple screenshots of different website sections.

faq.question: Will converting to JPG make my screenshot blurry?
faq.answer: Not if you use good conversion settings. Most tools let you choose quality levels. For website screenshots, high-quality JPG conversion keeps everything clear and readable while still reducing file size.

faq.question: Is this useful for social media posts?
faq.answer: Very useful. Social media platforms often work better with JPG files. Converting your website screenshot to JPG before posting can make it upload faster and display better on platforms like Facebook, Twitter, or Instagram.

title: How to Combine Multiple Images into One PDF Online (Step-by-Step)

faq.question: Why would I combine images into one PDF instead of sending them separately?
faq.answer: When you combine images into one PDF, everything stays together in the right order. The person receiving it doesn't have to open ten different files or try to figure out what goes where. It's just one clean file that opens easily on any device.

faq.question: What image formats can I combine into a PDF?
faq.answer: Most tools work with common image formats like JPG, PNG, and WebP. Basically, if you can view it as a picture on your computer or phone, you can probably combine it into a PDF.

faq.question: Will the image quality get worse when combined into PDF?
faq.answer: Not if you use the right settings. Good tools let you choose the quality level. For most purposes, you can keep the quality looking just fine while making the file size reasonable for sharing.

faq.question: Can I rearrange the images after uploading them?
faq.answer: Yes, that's the whole point of using a good combiner tool. You should be able to drag images around to get them in the exact order you want before creating the PDF.

faq.question: Is this useful for things like printing photo collages?
faq.answer: Very useful. Instead of printing ten separate photos and hoping they come out in order, you combine them into one PDF and print it as a single document. Everything stays together perfectly.

title: How to Rearrange PDF Pages Online Without Losing Quality

faq.question: Will rearranging PDF pages affect the quality of the document?
faq.answer: Not if you use a good tool. Quality PDF editors keep everything looking exactly the same—text stays sharp, images stay clear, formatting stays intact. The pages just end up in a different order.

faq.question: Can I extract just a few pages from a large PDF?
faq.answer: Yes, that's one of the most useful features. You can select specific pages you want to keep and create a new PDF with just those pages. It's perfect for when you only need part of a document.

faq.question: What if I need to merge pages from different PDFs?
faq.answer: Most good tools let you upload multiple PDFs and then mix and match pages from all of them. You can create a new document with pages from several different sources in whatever order makes sense.

faq.question: Is this safe for confidential documents?
faq.answer: If the tool processes files locally in your browser, your document never leaves your computer. Always check how a tool handles files before uploading sensitive documents.

faq.question: Can I undo changes if I make a mistake?
faq.answer: Yes, good tools let you reset and start over if you need to. You can usually drag pages back to their original positions or clear everything and begin again.

title: PDF Orientation Explained: Portrait vs Landscape (When to Use Each)
description: Practical guide explaining when to use portrait vs landscape orientation in PDFs. Learn from real-world examples and common mistakes to make your documents look right every time.

steps: Look at your content and decide if it's taller or wider
Choose portrait for text-heavy documents and reading
Choose landscape for spreadsheets, tables, and wide visuals
Consider how people will view it (phone, computer, print)
Don't be afraid to mix orientations in one document

faq.question: What's the main difference between portrait and landscape?
faq.answer: Portrait is taller than wide – like holding your phone normally to read a message. Landscape is wider than tall – like turning your phone sideways to watch a video. In practice, portrait works better for reading text line by line, while landscape works better for wide content like spreadsheets or photos.

faq.question: Will changing orientation affect my PDF quality?
faq.answer: Not at all. Changing orientation just rotates how the content is displayed. All your text, images, and formatting stay exactly the same quality – they're just arranged differently on the page. It's like turning a painting on the wall: the painting doesn't change, just which way it faces.

faq.question: What happens if I choose the wrong orientation?
faq.answer: The content gets cut off when printing, or looks awkward on screen. Text might wrap strangely, images get squeezed, and people have to zoom and scroll more than they should. It's annoying but easy to fix. Most PDF tools let you rotate pages or change orientation after the fact.

faq.question: Can I have both orientations in one PDF?
faq.answer: Yes, and this is something more people should use. You can have some pages in portrait and others in landscape within the same PDF. This is perfect for reports that mix text pages (portrait) with wide charts (landscape). The document flows naturally and each page shows its content in the best possible way.

faq.question: Does orientation matter for digital viewing?
faq.answer: It matters more than people think. On phones, portrait orientation usually works better because that's how people hold their devices. On computers and tablets, it depends on the content. Wide tables need landscape even on screens. The key is to think about how people will actually view the document, not just how it prints.

title: JPG vs WebP vs PNG: Which Image Format Actually Works Best for Websites in 2026
description: Plain talk about choosing image formats for websites. No technical jargon - just what works, what doesn't, and when to use each format for real websites.

breadcrumbs.name: JPG vs WebP vs PNG Guide

steps: Look at your image - is it photo, graphic, or has transparency?
Photos usually go JPG or WebP
Logos and graphics with transparency need PNG
Test WebP first for modern browsers
Always provide fallback formats

faq.question: Should I still use JPG in 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, absolutely. JPG works everywhere - every browser, every device, every email client. WebP is smaller and better quality, but if someone opens your site on an old phone or in a work computer with restrictions, JPG still works. I usually provide WebP first, with JPG as backup for compatibility.

faq.question: When should I definitely use PNG?
faq.answer: Three situations: when you need transparency (logos, icons with see-through backgrounds), when you have simple graphics with few colors (charts, diagrams, text overlays), or when you need perfect quality and file size doesn't matter (like a product photo where every detail counts). PNG handles transparency perfectly and doesn't compress graphics into blurry messes.

faq.question: Is WebP really better than JPG?
faq.answer: For modern websites, yes. WebP files are typically smaller than JPGs at the same quality. That means your site loads faster, which visitors and search engines both like. But here's the practical part: WebP doesn't work everywhere yet. Some older browsers, some email clients, some content management systems still struggle with it. So you use WebP, but you also provide JPG fallbacks.

faq.question: Do image formats affect SEO?
faq.answer: Indirectly, but yes. Smaller images mean faster loading, and page speed matters for SEO. But more importantly, the right format prevents problems. I've seen sites where PNG photos were so large they timed out on mobile, hurting their rankings. I've also seen sites using JPG for logos that looked terrible with white backgrounds. Choose the right format, compress properly, and your SEO benefits from the better user experience.

faq.question: What's the simplest rule for choosing?
faq.answer: Photos: try WebP first, JPG as backup. Graphics with transparency: PNG. Simple illustrations: PNG or SVG. Screenshots: PNG usually works better. And always test on actual devices - your phone, an old tablet, different browsers. What looks perfect on your computer might break on someone else's.

title: How to Compress JPG Images Online Without Losing Quality (2026 Guide)
description: Practical guide to reducing JPG file sizes while keeping photos looking good. Learn what actually works, what doesn't, and how to avoid common compression mistakes.

steps: Upload your JPG image to a compression tool
Start with moderate compression (70-85% quality)
Compare before/after preview
Download and check on your actual device
Keep original as backup

faq.question: Is it really possible to compress without losing quality?
faq.answer: Yes, but with an important clarification: you can compress significantly without noticing quality loss. JPG compression always removes some data, but our eyes don't notice small changes. A photo that's 90% smaller might look identical to the original for most purposes. The trick is finding that sweet spot where file size drops dramatically but visual quality stays acceptable.

faq.question: How much can I actually compress a JPG?
faq.answer: It depends completely on the image. Photos with lots of detail and textures (like landscapes) compress better than simple graphics. A typical photo might compress to 30-40% of its original size with no visible difference. For web use, you can often go to 20-30% without problems. The key is to compress, then actually look at the result on the screen where it will be used.

faq.question: What's the biggest mistake people make?
faq.answer: Compressing too much in one go. If you need a 500KB photo to be under 100KB, don't jump straight to maximum compression. Do it in stages. Compress to 300KB, check. Then to 200KB, check. Then to 100KB. Each time you compress a JPG, you lose more quality. Multiple compressions of the same file create artifacts - those blocky, blurry areas that make photos look terrible.

faq.question: Should I compress before or after editing?
faq.answer: Always compress last, after all your editing is done. If you compress, then resize, then add text, then compress again - each step degrades quality. Do your cropping, adjusting, resizing, and any edits first. Then compress once as the final step before uploading or sending.

faq.question: Do I need special software to compress JPGs?
faq.answer: Not anymore. Modern online tools work just as well as desktop software for most needs. The advantage of online tools is they're usually free, work on any device, and don't require installation. Just make sure you're using a tool that lets you preview the results and adjust the compression level - that control is essential.

title: How to Resize Passport Size Photo Online (2x2, 3.5x4.5 cm) - 2026 Guide
description: Step-by-step guide to resize photos for passports, visas, and ID cards. Get the exact dimensions right for USA, India, UK, Canada, Australia and more. Free online tool included.

steps: Take or select a good quality headshot photo
Choose your country's passport size requirements
Upload to online passport photo tool
Adjust background color if needed
Download print-ready passport photos

faq.question: What are the exact passport photo sizes for different countries?
faq.answer: USA: 2x2 inches (51x51 mm). India: 35x35 mm (3.5x3.5 cm). UK: 35x45 mm (3.5x4.5 cm). Canada: 50x70 mm (5x7 cm). Australia: 35x45 mm. Europe/Schengen: 35x45 mm. The exact dimensions matter - being off by even a few millimeters can get your application rejected. Most online tools have these sizes pre-configured.

faq.question: Can I use a selfie for my passport photo?
faq.answer: Yes, but with important rules. It needs to be recent (last 6 months), taken against a plain light background, with neutral expression and eyes open. No hats, glasses (unless medically required), or headphones. Face should fill about 70-80% of the frame. Most importantly, someone else should take it - selfies often have wrong angles and perspectives that get rejected.

faq.question: What's the difference between 300 DPI and 600 DPI?
faq.answer: DPI (dots per inch) affects print quality. 300 DPI is standard for most passport photos - it looks sharp and professional. 600 DPI is for when you need absolutely perfect quality, but the files are larger. For 99% of applications, 300 DPI is perfect. The key is that whatever DPI you choose, the physical dimensions (2x2 inches, 35x45mm) must still be correct.

faq.question: Why was my passport photo rejected?
faq.answer: Common reasons: wrong size (even slightly off), wrong background color (not plain light), shadows on face or background, incorrect head size (too small or too large), poor lighting, wearing glasses or hat, smiling too much, photo too old, or print quality issues. Online tools help avoid these by enforcing the exact requirements for each country.

faq.question: How many passport photos should I print per sheet?
faq.answer: It depends on your needs. If you just need one for your application, print 2-4 copies (officials often ask for extras). If you need photos for multiple documents (passport, visa, work permit, etc.), print 8-16. Most online tools let you choose how many copies per sheet, from 1 to 54. A4 or Letter paper with multiple photos is the most cost-effective.

title: How to Split PDF into Separate Pages Online (1 Page = 1 PDF) - 2026 Guide
description: Step-by-step guide to split PDF files into individual pages. Create separate PDFs for each page quickly and easily. Perfect for sharing specific pages, organizing documents, or extracting content.

steps: Upload your multi-page PDF file

Download all pages as individual files or ZIP
Share or use the single-page PDFs as needed

faq.question: Why would I need each page as a separate PDF?
faq.answer: There are many practical reasons: sharing just one page from a report without sending the whole document, uploading specific pages to different online systems that accept only single-page PDFs, organizing documents where each page needs to be filed separately, or when you need to print or email specific pages without the rest of the document. It gives you more control over your content.

faq.question: Will splitting affect the quality of my PDF pages?
faq.answer: No, splitting doesn't reduce quality at all. Each page becomes an exact copy of the original page. All text remains sharp, images stay at their original resolution, and formatting is preserved. It's like taking a book and carefully removing each page to create separate booklets - the content on each page doesn't change.

faq.question: What happens to bookmarks and links when I split?
faq.answer: This depends on the tool you use. Some advanced tools preserve bookmarks and internal links within each page, but links that point to other pages in the original document will break since those pages are now in separate files. For most uses, this doesn't matter since you're typically splitting to share or organize individual pages.

faq.question: Can I split only specific pages, not all of them?
faq.answer: Yes, most good splitting tools let you choose which pages to extract. You can select page ranges (like pages 3-7) or pick individual pages (like page 1, 5, and 9). You don't have to split every page if you don't need to. This is useful when you only want to separate a few important pages from a larger document.

faq.question: How do I handle many single-page PDFs after splitting?
faq.answer: Good tools give you a ZIP file containing all the individual pages, neatly organized. Without a ZIP, you'd have to download dozens of files separately. With a ZIP, you get one download that contains everything. You can then extract the ZIP to get all your single-page PDFs organized in a folder on your computer.

title: How to Convert PDF to JPG and PNG Without Blurry Quality (2026 Fix Guide)
description: Step-by-step guide to convert PDF pages to high-quality JPG and PNG images. Learn the exact settings for DPI, resolution, and format to avoid blurry, pixelated results every time.

steps: Upload your PDF file to a converter tool
Choose JPG or PNG output format
Select 300 DPI resolution (minimum for quality)
Adjust image quality to High
Download individual images or ZIP file

faq.question: Why do my converted PDF images always look blurry?
faq.answer: Almost always it's because of low DPI settings. Most converters default to 72 or 96 DPI for web, which looks fine on screens but pixelates when you zoom. Text becomes fuzzy, edges get rough, and images lose detail. The fix is simple: use 300 DPI for crisp text and clear graphics. For documents with fine details, 600 DPI gives you professional quality. 150 DPI is okay for drafts and quick previews, but not for anything you want to look good.

faq.question: What's the difference between JPG and PNG for PDF conversion?
faq.answer: JPG gives you smaller files but can introduce compression artifacts - those blocky, blurry areas especially around text and sharp edges. It's fine for photos and documents where tiny imperfections don't matter. PNG preserves every pixel exactly. Text stays razor sharp, graphics keep their crisp edges, and there's no quality loss. The tradeoff is file size - PNG files are usually much larger. Use PNG for screenshots, presentations, and documents where text clarity matters. Use JPG for photos and when file size is more important than perfect quality.

faq.question: Can I rotate pages during conversion?
faq.answer: Yes, good PDF to image converters let you rotate pages individually or in batch. This is essential when you scan documents that are upside down or sideways. Instead of converting then rotating each image separately, you can do it all in one step. Some tools let you rotate all pages at once (90° right, 90° left, 180°), others give you per-page control. Both save you from editing 50 images one by one.

faq.question: What DPI should I use for different purposes?
faq.answer: 150 DPI: Okay for quick drafts, previews, and documents that will only be viewed on screen at normal size. Text is readable but not sharp. 300 DPI: The sweet spot. Looks great on screens, prints well, files are manageable. Use this for most conversions. 600 DPI: Professional quality. Text is extremely sharp, graphics are crystal clear. Files are large. Use when you need maximum quality for printing, zooming, or archiving. For most people, 300 DPI is perfect.

faq.question: How do I download many converted images efficiently?
faq.answer: Don't download them one by one. A 50-page PDF converted to images means 50 individual files. Clicking each one is painful. Look for converters that offer ZIP downloads. This packages all your images into one compressed folder. One click, one download, then you extract the ZIP and all your images are neatly organized. Some tools also let you download just the current page view if you only need a few pages at a time.

title: How to Remove Blank Pages from PDF Online (2026 Step-by-Step Guide)
description: Simple guide to delete blank pages from any PDF. Stop scrolling through empty pages when printing or sharing. Works for scanned documents, downloaded reports, and merged files.

steps: Upload your PDF to a tool with blank page detection
Preview all pages and see which ones are blank
Select the blank pages you want to remove
Delete them in one click
Download the cleaned PDF

faq.question: How do blank pages get into my PDF?
faq.answer: Three main ways. One: Scanning. Office scanners often insert blank pages when they detect 'empty space' between documents. Two: Printing. When you print double-sided and a section ends on an odd page, a blank even page gets added. Three: Merging. When you combine PDFs, sometimes blank separator pages sneak in. Four: Exporting. PowerPoint and Word exports sometimes add blank pages at the end for no reason. All annoying, all fixable.

faq.question: Will I lose content if I accidentally delete the wrong page?
faq.answer: Good tools let you preview before deleting. You can see exactly what's on each page - blank vs not blank. Some even auto-detect blank pages and highlight them. Always preview first. Also, keep your original file as backup. If you accidentally delete something important, you can start over. Never upload your only copy anywhere.

faq.question: What counts as a 'blank page'?
faq.answer: Completely white with no text, images, or graphics. But sometimes pages look blank but actually have white text, tiny watermarks, or hidden metadata. Good tools detect these and warn you. Also, pages with just a page number or a small logo are not truly blank - they have content. Decide if you want to keep those based on your needs.

faq.question: Can I remove blank pages from scanned PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes. This is actually the most common use case. Scanned documents often have blank pages because the scanner detected two pages as one, or someone flipped too many pages. Online tools work fine on scanned PDFs since they're just images. The blank detection still works because it looks for white/empty areas.

faq.question: Do I need special software or can I do this online?
faq.answer: Online tools work perfectly for this. You don't need Adobe Acrobat or any paid software. Just upload, preview, delete, download. The best ones process everything in your browser - no server uploads, so your documents stay private. Takes less than a minute for most files.

faq.question: Should I download an app or just use a website on my phone?
faq.answer: Honestly? Depends on what you're doing. Apps work offline and are faster. Websites don't take up storage space. I use both. If I'm on train with spotty signal, I use an app. If I'm at home with good wifi, I just open a website. Apps are better if you merge PDFs regularly. Websites are fine for once in a while.

faq.question: Are these free PDF mergers safe? Will someone steal my documents?

Your files stay on your phone. Some other sites upload to their servers. They usually delete after an hour, but still. Read the privacy policy. If it sounds sketchy, don't use it.

faq.question: Why do some PDF mergers ask me to sign up?
faq.answer: They want your email so they can spam you later. Or they want to sell your data. Or they want you to forget and start paying after free trial ends. Good tools don't need your email. PDFSwift doesn't ask. Smallpdf free version doesn't ask. If a site demands signup before merging one file, close the tab.

faq.question: My PDFs are huge. Will merging work on phone?

Offline apps handle bigger files because they use your phone's processor. If you have massive PDFs, compress them first, then merge. Or use PDFSwift on desktop - handles bigger files than phones usually.

faq.question: Can I merge PDFs on my phone without internet?
faq.answer: Yes, but only with an app. PDF Merge: Scan, Sign & Edit for Android works offline. PDF Combiner for iPhone works without wifi. Websites need internet. So if you're traveling or have bad signal, get an app. Just check reviews first - some free apps are full of ads.

title: How to Add Pages to PDF Online (Insert Blank Page or Extra Page) – 2026 Guide
description: Need to add a blank page or insert an extra page into your PDF? Here's how to do it online without installing anything. Works on phone and computer.

steps: Upload your PDF to an online editor

Select 'Add Blank Page' or upload a file to insert
Position it before or after existing pages
Download your updated PDF

faq.question: Why would I need to add a blank page to a PDF?
faq.answer: Lots of reasons. You're printing a booklet and need an extra page to make the page count even. You want to add notes between sections. You're creating a workbook and need space for answers. You scanned something and realized you missed a page. You're merging documents and need a separator. Blank pages aren't just empty space - they're useful when you need them.

faq.question: Can I add a page from another PDF?
faq.answer: Yes, that's actually more common than adding blank pages. Most online tools let you upload a second PDF and insert specific pages from it into your main document. You can take page 3 from one file and insert it after page 5 in another. Or take all pages from a second PDF and append them at the end. Works like merging, but with more control over exactly where each page goes.

faq.question: Will adding pages mess up my formatting?
faq.answer: No. PDFs are fixed format - adding a page doesn't change anything on existing pages. Your text stays where it is, images don't shift, page numbers don't automatically update (that's the one thing to watch for). The new page just slides in. Everything else stays exactly the same.

faq.question: What about page numbers? Will they update automatically?
faq.answer: This is the catch. If your PDF has page numbers printed on each page (like "Page 1 of 20" at the bottom), those won't update when you add a new page. They're part of the content, not automatic. After inserting a page, page 3 still says "Page 3" even if it's now actually page 4. If correct numbering matters, you'll need to edit those numbers or live with the mismatch. Some tools can renumber pages, but it's not automatic.

faq.question: Can I add pages on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes. Most online PDF editors work in mobile browsers just fine. PDFSwift works on any phone - upload from your gallery or files, add pages, save back to your phone. No app needed. If you do this a lot, some people prefer apps, but for occasional use, a website is easier.

faq.question: Why do so many PDF to JPG tools add watermarks?
faq.answer: Money. Plain and simple. They give you a free version with a big ugly watermark across your image, then charge you to remove it. It's their business model. Some even watermark free trials so badly the image is useless. The good tools don't do this. They're either completely free (like PDFSwift) or have generous free tiers without watermarks.

faq.question: What's the best quality setting for PDF to JPG?
faq.answer: 300 DPI is the sweet spot. 72 DPI (what many free tools default to) looks fine on screen but pixelates when you zoom or print. 150 DPI is okay for drafts. 300 DPI gives you sharp text, clear images, and files that aren't huge. For professional printing, 600 DPI exists but files get massive. Stick with 300 DPI for 99% of uses.

faq.question: Will I lose quality converting PDF to JPG?
faq.answer: Yes, technically JPG is lossy compression. But with good settings, you won't notice. At 300 DPI and high quality setting, the difference is invisible to the naked eye. The real quality loss happens when tools use low resolution defaults. Your PDF might have vector text that's infinitely sharp, but if the converter spits out 72 DPI JPGs, it'll look terrible. Always check the DPI setting.

faq.question: Can I convert all pages at once?
faq.answer: Yes. Good tools convert every page to individual JPGs. A 20-page PDF becomes 20 JPG files. Most tools then let you download them all as a ZIP file so you don't have to click 20 times. One click, one download, all your images. If a tool makes you download pages one by one, find a better tool.

faq.question: Is it safe to upload sensitive PDFs to online converters?
faq.answer: Depends on the tool. Some upload your file to their servers, convert it there, then delete after an hour. That's okay for non-sensitive stuff. For private documents, use tools that work entirely in your browser (client-side). PDFSwift processes everything locally - your file never leaves your computer. No upload means no one else can access it.

faq.question: Do I really need Adobe to add images to PDF?

PDFSwift, Lumin, Smallpdf, iLovePDF - all work without Adobe. Some even work completely in your browser so your files never get uploaded anywhere. Adobe is fine if you already have it, but don't buy it just to add a photo.

faq.question: What image formats work best?
faq.answer: JPG for photos. PNG if you need transparency or have logos with sharp text. Most tools support both. GIF works too but quality isn't great. If your image is huge (like 10MB+), resize it first or your PDF file size will blow up. 300 DPI is plenty for most uses.

faq.question: Will the image cover up my text?
faq.answer: That depends on where you put it. Most editors let you drag images anywhere. If you place it over text, it'll cover it. You can usually adjust layering - some tools let you send image behind text or adjust transparency. For signatures, you want it on top. For watermarks, you might want it faded behind. Know what you need before you start.

faq.question: Can I add multiple images at once?
faq.answer: Most tools make you add them one by one. You can add 20 images, but you'll click 'add image' 20 times. Some desktop apps let you batch insert, but online tools usually don't. If you need to add dozens of images, you might be better off converting the whole thing to Word, adding images there, and converting back to PDF.

faq.question: Is it safe to upload my PDF to these tools?
faq.answer: This is the real question. Some tools upload your file to their servers. They usually delete after an hour, but still - it's on their computer. For personal stuff, fine. For bank statements, contracts, IDs - use tools that process in your browser. PDFSwift does this. Safe2PDF does this. Your file never leaves your device. Always check before uploading sensitive documents.

title: PDF to JPG Looks Blurry? Here Are the Best Resolution Settings
description: Tired of blurry JPGs from your PDFs? Here's exactly which resolution settings to use and why. No guesswork. Just clear images every time.

steps: Open your PDF in a converter that lets you change DPI settings
Set resolution to 300 DPI for most uses (150 DPI for web only)
Choose JPG with quality set to 85-90% (not 100%)
Convert a single page first to test
Zoom to 100% and check if text is sharp
Adjust up or down based on your needs

faq.question: What DPI should I use for PDF to JPG conversion?
faq.answer: 300 DPI is the sweet spot for almost everything. It gives you sharp text, clear images, and files that aren't huge. 150 DPI is fine for web use where file size matters more. 72 DPI (the default on many tools) is why your images look blurry - it's too low. 600 DPI exists but files get massive and you probably don't need it unless you're printing professionally [citation:1][citation:3][citation:9].

faq.question: Why does my PDF to JPG conversion look pixelated?
faq.answer: Almost always because of low DPI. Most free converters default to 72 DPI to keep files small and processing fast. But 72 DPI means every inch of your image only has 72 pixels. When you have text or fine lines, there aren't enough pixels to make them sharp. The fix: use 300 DPI. Also check if you're compressing too much - JPG quality below 70% starts to show artifacts [citation:4][citation:10].

faq.question: Is PNG better than JPG for PDF conversion?
faq.answer: For text and sharp graphics, yes. PNG is lossless - no compression artifacts. Text stays razor sharp. JPG is lossy - it throws away detail to save space. For photos, JPG is fine because the loss is hard to see. For documents with small text, diagrams, or logos, PNG is better. The tradeoff: PNG files are 3-5x larger [citation:7][citation:9].

faq.question: Will 300 DPI always give perfect results?
faq.answer: No. 300 DPI is the right setting, but if your original PDF has low-quality images, no setting can fix that. Garbage in, garbage out. Also, if you set JPG quality to 100%, files get huge with almost no visible improvement over 90%. The best results come from a good source PDF + 300 DPI + 85-90% quality [citation:1][citation:10].

faq.question: Do online PDF to JPG tools respect my privacy?
faq.answer: Some do, some don't. Tools that process in your browser (client-side) never upload your file anywhere - that's safest. Tools that upload to servers usually delete after an hour, but your file sat on their computer. For sensitive documents, use browser-based tools like PDFSwift or PDF24, or offline software like ImageMagick [citation:10].

title: Merge PDF in Correct Order (Avoid Page Mix-Up) – Simple Guide
description: Tired of merged PDFs with pages in the wrong order? Here's how to arrange files correctly before merging. No more page mix-ups.

steps: Upload all PDF files you want to merge
Look at the list of files – this is your order
Drag and drop to rearrange (File A first, File B second)
Check preview thumbnails if available
Click merge and download

faq.question: Why do my merged PDF pages always end up in the wrong order?
faq.answer: Because most tools merge files in the order you select them, not the order you think. If you click File B first then File A, File B becomes page 1. It's easy to mix up. Always check the list before merging. Good tools let you drag files to rearrange. Bad tools just merge in selection order and don't let you change it. Look for drag-and-drop ordering.

faq.question: Can I rearrange pages after merging?
faq.answer: Yes, but it's extra work. You'd need a separate 'reorder pages' tool. Better to get the order right before merging. Some PDF mergers show page thumbnails so you can see exactly what you're getting. That's the safest way – preview then merge.

faq.question: What if I have 20 files to merge? How do I keep track?
faq.answer: Name your files with numbers first: 01_intro.pdf, 02_chapter1.pdf, 03_chapter2.pdf. Then when you upload, they'll appear in alphabetical order. Most file pickers sort by name. This trick saves so much time. If your tool supports drag-drop, you can also rearrange visually.

faq.question: Do all PDF mergers let me rearrange files?
faq.answer: No. Free versions of some tools just merge in the order you select and don't let you change it. That's where mistakes happen. Look for tools that show a list of uploaded files and let you drag them up/down. PDFSwift does this. Smallpdf does this. iLovePDF does this. Avoid tools that merge immediately without letting you reorder.

faq.question: What's the most common page order mistake?
faq.answer: Append vs Insert confusion. Most tools 'append' – they add new files at the end. If you want File B to go before File A, you need to upload them in reverse order. Or use a tool that lets you insert between existing files. The mistake: uploading in logical order (A then B) but wanting B first. Always check the list before clicking merge.

title: How to Merge PDF for Exam Forms & Certificates (Step-by-Step)
description: Applying for exams? Need to combine forms, certificates, and ID proofs? Here's exactly how to merge PDFs for applications without messing up the order.

steps: Gather all your documents: application form, certificates, ID proof, photo, signature
Name files in order: 01_form.pdf, 02_marksheet.pdf, 03_id.pdf etc.
Upload all files to a PDF merger (PDFSwift or similar)
Arrange them in required order (check exam instructions)
Merge and download one combined PDF
Check file size – if too large, compress before uploading

faq.question: What's the standard order for exam application documents?
faq.answer: Every exam is different, but common order: 1) Filled application form, 2) Educational certificates (10th, 12th, graduation in order), 3) ID proof (Aadhar, passport, etc.), 4) Photo, 5) Signature, 6) Payment receipt. Always check your exam's instructions – some have very specific requirements. Following their order exactly prevents rejection.

faq.question: My files are too big to upload. What should I do?
faq.answer: This is the biggest problem with exam forms. Certificates scanned at high resolution can be 5-10MB each. Three certificates plus form plus ID = 50MB file. Many exam portals limit to 2MB or 5MB. Fix: Compress PDFs first. Use PDFSwift Compress tool or Smallpdf. Reduce to 1-2MB per file. Keep quality readable but don't go below 150 DPI. Also scan in black & white, not color – smaller files.

faq.question: Do I need to sign documents digitally before merging?
faq.answer: Yes, most exams want your signature on the form. Options: Sign on paper, scan it, then merge. Or use a digital signature tool to add signature directly to PDF. Some exams accept typed names, but physical signature is safer. If you have a signature image (photo of your signature), you can add it to the PDF before merging using PDFSwift's 'Add Image' feature.

faq.question: What if my certificates are in different formats (JPG, PNG)?
faq.answer: Convert them to PDF first. Most scanners save as JPG. Use PDFSwift Image to PDF tool. Convert all images to PDF, then merge. This is better than trying to merge mixed formats. Also, photos and signatures should be in separate PDF pages, not embedded in forms. Each document gets its own page.

faq.question: The exam portal says my merged PDF is corrupted. Why?
faq.answer: Usually two reasons: File too large (compress it), or special characters in filename (use simple names like form.pdf, not form#2026@final.pdf). Also, some online mergers produce PDFs that aren't fully compatible. Use trusted tools. If problem persists, try merging in smaller batches or use Adobe's free online tool.

title: PNG vs JPG: Which One is Better for Website Speed & SEO? (2026 Guide)
description: Compare PNG vs JPG for website performance and SEO. Learn which image format is best for photos, graphics, and Core Web Vitals in 2026.

steps: Identify your image types: photos (use JPG/WebP), logos/graphics (use PNG/WebP)
Check current image formats on your site using tools like Screaming Frog or GTmetrix
Convert photos from PNG to JPG (reduces size 5-10x)
Convert all images to WebP for best performance (use Squoosh or image optimization plugins)
Implement picture element with fallbacks for older browsers
Verify Core Web Vitals improvement after optimization

faq.question: Is PNG or JPG better for SEO?
faq.answer: JPG is generally better for SEO than PNG because it produces smaller file sizes for photographs, leading to faster page load times. However, for logos and graphics, PNG may be necessary. The best SEO practice is to use WebP with appropriate fallbacks.

faq.question: Does Google prefer WebP over JPG?
faq.answer: Google doesn't explicitly prefer one format over another, but it does prioritize page speed. Since WebP offers the best compression-to-quality ratio, using it indirectly helps your SEO by improving Core Web Vitals.

faq.question: Can I convert all my JPG/PNG images to WebP?
faq.answer: Yes, you can and should convert most of your images to WebP. Use the <picture> element to serve WebP to modern browsers while providing JPG/PNG fallbacks for older browsers. Tools like Squoosh, CloudConvert, or image optimization plugins can batch convert your images.

faq.question: What about file size limits for website images?
faq.answer: Aim for under 100KB for most web images, under 200KB for hero images. JPGs at 60-80% quality usually hit this. PNGs often exceed these limits, which is why they're not ideal for web photos. WebP can achieve similar quality at 30-50% smaller file sizes.

faq.question: Should I use PNG for retina/high-DPI displays?
faq.answer: No. Use SVG for logos/icons (vector = perfect scaling) or WebP at 2x resolution. PNG files become massive at high resolutions. Better to serve responsive images with srcset than rely on PNG for sharpness.

title: Remove First / Last Page from PDF Easily (2026 Guide)
description: Need to delete the first or last page from a PDF? Here's how to remove pages instantly without Adobe. Works on phone and computer. Free, no signup.

steps: Upload your PDF to a page remover tool (PDFSwift or similar)
Select which pages to keep (or which to remove)
For first page: keep pages 2 to end
For last page: keep pages 1 to second-last
For multiple pages: specify ranges like 1-3,5,7-9
Click 'Remove Pages' and download your new PDF

faq.question: Can I remove the first page of a PDF for free?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free. Tools like PDFSwift, iLovePDF, and Smallpdf let you delete pages without paying. No signup required. Just upload, select pages to keep, and download. Takes 30 seconds.

faq.question: How do I delete the last page of a PDF?
faq.answer: Upload your PDF to any page remover tool. You'll see thumbnails of all pages. Either click the last page and hit delete, or specify the page range to keep (e.g., keep pages 1-9 if your PDF has 10 pages). Download the result.

faq.question: Will the page numbers stay correct after removing pages?
faq.answer: Yes, the PDF renumbers automatically. If you remove page 1, the old page 2 becomes the new page 1. Page numbers in the document content (like headers) won't change – that's part of the PDF content itself. But the actual page order renumbers correctly.

faq.question: What if I need to remove multiple pages from the middle?
faq.answer: Same process. Most tools let you specify ranges like '1-5,8,10-15' to keep only certain pages. Or you can select multiple thumbnails while holding Ctrl (Cmd on Mac) and delete them all at once. Some tools also let you type in page numbers to remove.

faq.question: Is it safe to upload sensitive PDFs to remove pages?
faq.answer: For sensitive documents, use browser-based tools that process locally – files never leave your device. PDFSwift does this. Other tools upload to servers and delete after processing (usually 1-2 hours). For tax returns, contracts, IDs – use local processing tools.

title: Passport Photo for Indian Govt Exams (Exact Size) – 2026 Guide
description: Exact photo size, format, and specifications for UPSC, NEET, RRB, and other Indian government exams. Includes file size, background rules, and common rejection reasons.

steps: Check the exact photo requirements for your specific exam (UPSC, NEET, RRB, etc.)
Get a recent photo with plain white background and natural expression
Ensure face covers 70-80% of the frame (about 1 inch height from chin to forehead)
Scan or click photo in high resolution, save as JPG/JPEG only

faq.question: What is the exact photo size for UPSC 2026?
faq.answer: UPSC specifies file size between 20 KB to 200 KB in JPG format. They don't give exact pixel dimensions, but require face to cover about 75% (three-fourths) of the photograph. Background must be plain white, eyes open, both ears visible, neutral expression. Photo must be recent and you'll also need to capture a live photo during application [citation:4][citation:10].

faq.question: What are the photo requirements for NEET 2026?
faq.answer: NEET requires passport size photograph in JPG/JPEG format between 10 KB to 200 KB. White background, 80% face coverage, ears visible. You'll also need a separate postcard size photo (4" x 6") with same specifications [citation:3].

faq.question: What happens if my photo doesn't match the live capture?
faq.answer: For UPSC 2026, the uploaded photograph must match the live-captured image exactly. If there's any mismatch, the candidate will not be allowed to proceed with the application process. This is a new security measure introduced to prevent fraud and impersonation [citation:4][citation:10].

faq.question: Can I wear glasses in my exam photo?
faq.answer: Prescription glasses are allowed but must be clear and thin-framed with no flash reflection. Sunglasses or colored glasses are strictly prohibited. For RRB, ensure no glare on glasses [citation:1][citation:6]. UPSC also advises against glare on spectacles [citation:4].

faq.question: What dress should I wear for RRB exam photo?
faq.answer: RRB specifically requires dark-colored dress (not white) with plain white background. This ensures contrast between subject and background. Other exams don't specify dress color but avoid uniforms [citation:6].

faq.question: Is there a correction window if I upload wrong photo?
faq.answer: For UPSC 2026, there is NO correction window after submission. Errors in photographs or signatures will lead to outright rejection of the application and may invite disciplinary action. Verify everything before final submit [citation:2][citation:5][citation:10]. NEET offers a correction window in second week of March 2026 [citation:3].

Select 'All pages' or specify page ranges (e.g., 1-1 for first page only)

faq.question: What's the best way to split a PDF for email attachments?
faq.answer: If your PDF is too large for email (Gmail limit 25MB), use 'Split by file size' option. Set maximum file size to 20MB or 10MB. The tool automatically splits your PDF into chunks under that size. Alternatively, split every 10-20 pages depending on file size.

faq.question: How do I split a PDF for government exam applications?
faq.answer: Most government portals (UPSC, SSC, RRB) require each document separately – photo, signature, 10th marksheet, 12th marksheet, graduation certificate, etc. First merge related pages into one PDF, then extract each document as separate PDF. Name files as required (e.g., 'photo.pdf', 'signature.pdf').

faq.question: Will splitting a PDF reduce quality?
faq.answer: No, splitting doesn't compress or reduce quality. Each page becomes its own PDF with the same resolution and content as the original. The file size per page stays the same – if your original 10-page PDF is 5MB, each page will be roughly 0.5MB.

faq.question: Can I split a PDF on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, works on iPhone and Android. Open any online PDF splitter in your mobile browser. Upload from phone storage or cloud. Select split options. Download individual PDFs or ZIP file. Some tools like PDFSwift work entirely in browser – no app download needed.

faq.question: What if I need to split a password-protected PDF?
faq.answer: You need the password first. Remove protection using a PDF unlocker tool (requires password), then split the unprotected PDF. Most splitter tools can't open locked PDFs without password entry first.

title: Remove Pages from Scanned PDF (Without Re-scan) – 2026 Guide

faq.question: Can I remove pages from a scanned PDF without Adobe Acrobat?
faq.answer: Yes, absolutely. You don't need Adobe Acrobat to delete pages from scanned PDFs. Free online tools like Smallpdf, PDF24, and PDFSwift let you remove pages directly in your browser. Desktop alternatives like Preview (Mac) or PDF24 Creator (Windows) work offline. No subscription needed [citation:1][citation:3][citation:6].

faq.question: How do I identify blank pages in a scanned PDF?
faq.answer: Upload your scanned PDF to any page removal tool – they show thumbnails of all pages. Blank pages appear as empty white squares. Click any thumbnail to see a larger preview – if nothing appears or only faint shadows/specks show, it's safe to delete. Some scanners leave faint marks that look like content, but if no visible text or images, delete it [citation:1][citation:7].

faq.question: Will removing pages affect the quality of remaining pages?
faq.answer: No. Deleting pages doesn't compress or re-encode the remaining pages. The pages you keep stay at their original scan resolution and quality. Your cleaned PDF will be smaller (fewer pages) but each kept page remains identical to the original [citation:1][citation:6].

faq.question: Can I delete multiple pages at once from a scanned PDF?
faq.answer: Yes. Most tools let you select multiple pages before deleting. Hold Ctrl (Windows) or Cmd (Mac) while clicking thumbnails to select several pages, then delete them all at once. Some tools also let you specify page ranges like '1-3,5,7-9' to keep or remove in bulk [citation:1][citation:7].

faq.question: Is it safe to upload scanned PDFs to online tools?
faq.answer: Most reputable tools are safe – they use TLS encryption and automatically delete files after processing (usually 1-2 hours). Smallpdf, for example, is GDPR-compliant and ISO/IEC 27001 certified [citation:1]. For sensitive documents, use browser-based tools that process locally (files never leave your device) or desktop software like Preview (Mac) or PDF24 Creator (Windows) that work completely offline [citation:3][citation:6].

faq.question: Can I remove pages from a password-protected scanned PDF?
faq.answer: Yes, but you need the password first. Most online tools will prompt for password when you upload a protected file. Once unlocked, you can delete pages normally. Alternatively, use a PDF unlocker tool first (requires password), then delete pages from the unprotected version [citation:1][citation:3].

faq.question: How do I remove pages on my phone?
faq.answer: Works on iPhone and Android. Open any online PDF tool in your mobile browser. Upload from phone storage, Google Drive, or Dropbox. Select pages to delete using thumbnails. Download cleaned PDF back to your phone. Takes about a minute – no app needed [citation:1][citation:6].

faq.question: Why do some websites save as WebP instead of JPG?
faq.answer: WebP is Google's modern image format that makes files 25-34% smaller than JPG while maintaining the same quality. Websites use it to load faster and save bandwidth. However, some apps and platforms don't support WebP yet, so conversion is still needed [citation:2].

faq.question: How do I convert WebP to JPG on iPhone without any apps?

The converted JPG saves in the same location [citation:2].

faq.question: How do I convert WebP to JPG on Android without third-party apps?

The copy will be saved as JPG automatically. This is a clever workaround since Google Photos converts edits to JPG [citation:2].

faq.question: Can I capture full webpage screenshots on desktop too?

faq.question: What's the difference between WebP and JPG quality?
faq.answer: WebP provides the same visual quality as JPG but with 25-34% smaller file sizes. It also supports transparency (like PNG) and animation (like GIF). However, JPG has universal compatibility – every device and app can open it. For screenshots you plan to share or upload, JPG is safer unless you know the platform supports WebP [citation:2].

faq.question: Are online WebP converters safe?

For sensitive images, use offline methods (Paint/Preview) or on-device conversion.

faq.question: How do I split a PDF into 2 equal parts?
faq.answer: Upload your PDF to a splitter tool like PDFSwift. Choose 'Split by number of sections' and enter 2. The tool will divide the document into two halves. If the PDF has an odd number of pages, the first half gets one extra page (e.g., 13 pages → 7 and 6) [citation:6][citation:8].

faq.question: Can I split a PDF into 3 equal parts for free?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free. Use online tools like PDFSwift, Aspose Splitter, or PDF Candy. Upload your file, select 'Split by number of sections' and enter 3. The tool automatically distributes pages into three files. For a 30-page PDF, you'll get three 10-page files [citation:2][citation:6].

faq.question: What if my PDF doesn't split evenly (e.g., 25 pages into 3 parts)?
faq.answer: Most tools handle this automatically. They distribute pages as evenly as possible – the first files get one extra page if needed. For 25 pages into 3 parts, you'll get files with 9, 8, and 8 pages. The content order is preserved, and no pages are lost [citation:8].

faq.question: Can I split a PDF into 5 parts on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, works on iPhone and Android. Open PDFSwift in your mobile browser, upload from phone storage, choose split by number of sections (5), and download the ZIP file. No app needed. For iPhone, you can also use the Files app with built-in PDF tools [citation:4].

faq.question: Is there a file size limit for splitting PDFs?
faq.answer: Most free online tools accept up to 50-100MB per file [citation:4][citation:8]. For larger files, use desktop software like PDF Candy Desktop or Stirling-PDF which have no size limits. PDFSwift processes files locally in your browser, so very large files may be slower but still work [citation:9].

faq.question: Will splitting a PDF reduce quality?
faq.answer: No, splitting does not compress or re-encode the content. Each split file maintains the exact quality and resolution of the original. Pages are simply copied into new files without any quality loss [citation:8].

faq.question: Can I split a password-protected PDF?
faq.answer: You need the password first. Most online tools will prompt for password when you upload a protected file. Enter the password, then split normally. If you don't have the password, you cannot edit or split the file [citation:8].

faq.question: Can I extract only odd or even pages from a PDF for free?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free. Online tools like Aspose and Sejda let you split PDFs by odd and even pages at no cost [citation:2]. For offline use, PDFsam Basic is free and open-source [citation:3]. Some tools have daily limits, but most are unlimited for basic splitting.

faq.question: Why would I need to separate odd and even pages?
faq.answer: The most common reason is double-sided printing – print odd pages first, then flip the paper and print even pages on the back [citation:10]. It's also useful when scanning double-sided documents, reorganizing files, or removing blank back pages from scanned documents.

faq.question: What happens if my PDF has an odd number of pages?
faq.answer: The odd pages file will contain pages 1,3,5... up to the last page. The even pages file will contain pages 2,4,6... up to the last even page. If you have 7 pages, odds will have 4 pages (1,3,5,7) and evens will have 3 pages (2,4,6).

faq.question: Is it safe to upload my PDF to online odd/even splitters?
faq.answer: Reputable tools like Aspose automatically delete your files from their servers after 24 hours [citation:2]. For maximum privacy, use offline tools like PDFsam Basic [citation:3] or browser-based tools like PDF Barber that process files locally without uploading [citation:4].

faq.question: Can I extract odd/even pages on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, online tools like Aspose and Sejda work on mobile browsers. Upload from your phone storage, select odd/even split mode, and download the ZIP file. No app needed [citation:2].

faq.question: What's the difference between splitting and extracting?
faq.answer: Splitting by odd/even creates two separate files – one with odd pages, one with even pages. Extracting usually means pulling specific pages into a new file. For odd/even separation, you're essentially splitting the document into two files based on page parity [citation:1][citation:5].

faq.question: Can I merge odd and even pages back together after extracting?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use merge tools to combine them back. Tools like Sejda offer 'Alternate & Mix' specifically for merging odd and even page files back into proper order [citation:10]. This is useful if you've scanned front/back pages separately.

faq.question: What is Gmail's attachment size limit in 2026?
faq.answer: Gmail still has a 25MB limit for attachments in 2026. If your PDF is larger than 25MB, Gmail will reject it and bounce back. Using PDFSwift, you can split your PDF into smaller parts under 20MB to stay safely under the limit.

faq.question: How do I split a PDF for email using PDFSwift?
faq.answer: It's simple: Go to PDFSwift Split PDF tool, upload your file (it stays in your browser – private and secure), choose 'Split by file size', set target to 20MB, click Split, and download your ZIP file. The whole process takes less than a minute.

faq.question: Is PDFSwift really free? Any hidden charges?
faq.answer: PDFSwift is completely free forever. No signup, no subscription, no hidden charges. You can split as many PDFs as you want, any size, any time. We believe PDF tools should be free and accessible to everyone.

faq.question: Is it safe to upload my PDF to PDFSwift?
faq.answer: PDFSwift processes files locally in your browser – they never leave your device. No upload, no servers, no third-party access. Your documents stay 100% private. This is especially important for sensitive emails containing contracts, invoices, or personal information.

faq.question: Can I split PDF on my phone for email?
faq.answer: Yes! PDFSwift works perfectly on iPhone and Android. Open Safari (iPhone) or Chrome (Android), go to PDFSwift, upload from your phone storage, split the PDF, and attach directly to email. No app installation needed.

faq.question: What if my PDF is 50MB? How many parts will I get?
faq.answer: With PDFSwift's 'Split by file size' set to 20MB, a 50MB PDF will split into 3 parts – approximately 17MB each. All parts are downloaded as a ZIP file. You can then send 3 separate emails or use cloud storage to share all parts at once.

faq.question: Can I split a password-protected PDF?
faq.answer: PDFSwift supports password-protected PDFs. You'll be prompted to enter the password when you upload. Once unlocked, you can split normally. If you don't have the password, the file cannot be processed.

faq.question: What's better – splitting or compressing for email?
faq.answer: Both! Use PDFSwift Compress PDF tool first to reduce file size. If it's still over 25MB, then split. This combination often results in fewer split files. For example, compress a 50MB PDF to 30MB, then split into 2 parts instead of 3.
